expectations.text: Lists at least three plausible state differences (different params name, missing populate, different upstream restriction, env / version drift) without committing to one cause
expectations.evidence: Lists all four numbered (params_name, populate not run, different upstream restriction, environment drift) and explicitly says 'Don't commit to one until you've inspected both sides'.

expectations.text: Recommends a concrete first diagnostic (e.g., inspecting (Selection & key).fetch() output side-by-side)
expectations.evidence: 'First diagnostic: inspect both users' Selection & key side-by-side' with concrete `(SortedSpikesDecodingSelection & key).fetch(as_dict=True)`.

expectations.text: Fetches from UserEnvironment and compares against the user's running env (sys.version, package versions); does not generic-suggest 'check your env'.
expectations.evidence: Concrete code uses `UserEnvironment().has_matching_env(env_id=THEIR_ENV_ID, show_diffs=True)` and `get_dep_version(...)` rather than a generic 'check your env'.

expectations.text: Does not claim the pipeline state must be identical when the symptoms prove otherwise
expectations.evidence: 'don't claim the two pipeline states must be identical — the symptom is direct evidence they aren't'.

expectations.text: Does not misframe cautious_delete as a write/populate gate — cautious_delete blocks deletes only
expectations.evidence: 'cautious_delete (the .delete() alias) is a deletion gate ... It does not gate writes or populate(), so it cannot cause fetch_results to return empty'.
